title: Isla and the Case of the Missing Treats
synopsis: When the animal treats go missing from the pet store, young detective Isla must solve the mystery with help from her diverse group of animal friends. Follow clues and discover surprising truths in this delightful mystery adventure.

pages.text:
Isla loved animals. She worked at Paws and Claws Pet Store with her mom. Every day, she helped feed the animals and clean their homes. The pets all knew Isla. They wagged, chirped, and purred when they saw her coming.

"Good morning, everyone!" Isla said. She had her curly black hair in two ponytails today. Her mom had given her a special detective badge to wear. "Time for breakfast!"

But when Isla opened the treat cabinet, her eyes went wide. "Oh no!" she gasped. "The treats are gone!"

illustration.description: A young Black girl with curly hair in ponytails stands shocked in front of an empty wooden cabinet in a colorful pet store. Various animals in cages look on with worried expressions - a parrot, rabbits, hamsters. The girl wears a small detective badge and bright clothes. The pet store has vibrant blues, greens, and yellows with natural wood tones.
illustration.alt: Isla discovers the empty treat cabinet while animals watch

pages.text:
All the animal treats were missing! The dog biscuits, the bird seeds, the rabbit pellets, and the fish flakes. Even the special catnip cookies were gone.

"Mom!" Isla called. "We have a mystery!"

Her mom, Mrs. Chen, came running. "What's wrong, detective?"

"Someone took all the treats," Isla said. She put her hands on her hips like a real detective. "But who? And why?"

The animals started making worried sounds. Coco the parrot squawked loudly. Benny the beagle howled. Even Whiskers the cat meowed sadly.

illustration.description: Isla stands with hands on hips in detective pose while her Asian mother looks concerned. Around them, various animals are making distressed sounds - a green parrot squawking, a brown beagle howling, an orange tabby cat meowing. The scene is dramatic but colorful, with warm oranges and reds showing the urgency of the situation.
illustration.alt: Isla takes charge as detective while animals and her mother react to the missing treats

pages.text:
"Don't worry," Isla told all the animals. "I will find your treats. I am Detective Isla, and I always solve mysteries!"

She got out her magnifying glass. It was pink with purple flowers. Her grandpa had given it to her for her birthday.

"First, I need to look for clues," Isla said. She bent down to examine the floor near the cabinet. "Aha! I see something!"

There were tiny brown crumbs on the floor. They made a trail leading toward the back door of the store.

illustration.description: Isla kneels on the floor with a decorative pink and purple magnifying glass, examining small brown crumbs. The crumbs form a visible trail across the colorful tile floor toward a door in the background. Her expression is focused and determined. The lighting creates a sense of discovery with golden highlights on the clues.
illustration.alt: Detective Isla examines crumbs on the floor with her magnifying glass

pages.text:
Isla followed the crumb trail outside. The morning sun was bright and warm. She walked slowly, looking carefully at each crumb.

"The trail goes to the park!" she said. "Whoever took the treats went this way."

In the park, Isla saw her friend Marcus. He was a tall boy with dark skin and a big smile. He was playing with his dog, Rosie.

"Hi, Marcus!" Isla called. "Did you see anyone carrying pet treats this morning?"

Marcus shook his head. "No, but Rosie was acting funny earlier. She kept running toward the pond."

illustration.description: Isla walks through a sunny park following a trail while talking to Marcus, a tall Black boy with a friendly smile, who stands with his golden retriever. The park has lush green grass, colorful flowers, and a pond visible in the background. The scene is bright and cheerful with blues and greens dominating the natural setting.
illustration.alt: Isla talks to Marcus and his dog Rosie in the sunny park

pages.text:
Isla and Marcus walked to the pond together. Rosie ran ahead, wagging her tail. At the pond, they met Elena. She had long brown hair and was feeding bread to the ducks.

"Elena!" Isla said. "Did you see anything strange this morning? Someone took all the pet treats from our store."

Elena pointed across the pond. "I saw a small animal by those trees. It was carrying something big. Too big for such a little creature!"

"What kind of animal?" Isla asked. She held up her magnifying glass to look at the trees.

illustration.description: By a serene blue pond, Isla holds her magnifying glass while talking to Elena, a Latina girl with long brown hair who holds bread for the white ducks swimming nearby. Marcus and Rosie stand beside them. Across the pond, tall green trees create a mysterious backdrop where the suspect might be hiding.
illustration.alt: The children gather by the pond as Elena points toward the trees

pages.text:
"It was brown and furry," Elena said. "It had a big fluffy tail. And it moved very fast!"

"A squirrel!" Marcus said. "But why would a squirrel take pet treats?"

Isla was thinking hard. She tapped her chin with her finger. "Maybe the squirrel was hungry. Or maybe..."

Sudenly, they heard a chattering sound from the trees. Then they heard barking, meowing, and chirping sounds too.

"Those sound like the animals from our store!" Isla said. "But how can that be? They're all in their homes at the shop."

illustration.description: The three children look puzzled and curious as they listen to various animal sounds coming from the dense green trees. Isla has her finger to her chin in a thinking pose. Sound waves are visually represented floating from the trees in colorful swirls of yellow and orange, suggesting the mysterious noises.
illustration.alt: The children listen to mysterious animal sounds coming from the forest

pages.text:
Isla, Marcus, and Elena crept toward the trees. They moved very quietly. Rosie stayed close behind them.

Behind the biggest oak tree, they found something amazing. There was a small brown squirrel surrounded by empty treat bags. But the squirrel was not alone!

There were three tiny baby squirrels. They were so small their eyes were barely open. The mama squirrel was trying to soften the hard dog biscuits with pond water.

"Oh!" Isla whispered. "She took the treats for her babies!"

illustration.description: Behind a large oak tree, a brown mother squirrel tends to three tiny baby squirrels while surrounded by colorful empty treat bags. The children peek around the tree with wonder and compassion. The scene is touching and warm, with dappled sunlight filtering through green leaves, creating a magical forest atmosphere.
illustration.alt: The children discover the mother squirrel feeding her babies with the stolen treats

pages.text:
The mama squirrel saw them and chattered nervously. She stood in front of her babies to protect them.

"It's okay," Isla said softly. "We won't hurt you. You just wanted to feed your family."

Isla sat down slowly. "I understand. You were hungry, and your babies needed food."

Marcus smiled. "The treats are too hard for baby squirrels though."

"That's true," Elena agreed. "Baby animals need special soft food."

Isla had an idea. Her eyes lit up with excitement. "I know how to help!"

illustration.description: Isla sits cross-legged on the forest floor, speaking gently to the protective mother squirrel while Marcus and Elena stand nearby with kind expressions. The mother squirrel looks less frightened, and sunbeams create a peaceful, understanding atmosphere. The colors are warm and soft, emphasizing the compassionate moment.
illustration.alt: Isla speaks kindly to the worried mother squirrel while her friends look on

pages.text:
Isla ran back to the pet store with Marcus and Elena. Her mom was still there, looking worried.

"Mom, I solved the mystery!" Isla said. "A mama squirrel took the treats for her babies. But they need different food."

Isla gathered soft kitten food, some nuts, and fresh fruit. "This will be much better for them."

They returned to the trees. The mama squirrel was still there. Isla placed the food nearby and stepped back.

The mama squirrel sniffed the food. Then she took some soft kitten food to her babies. They ate it right up!

illustration.description: Under the oak tree, Isla places bowls of appropriate food while the squirrel family cautiously approaches. The mother squirrel looks grateful as the baby squirrels eat the soft food successfully. The scene radiates warmth and satisfaction, with golden afternoon light and rich earth tones showing the happy resolution.
illustration.alt: The squirrel family enjoys the proper food that Isla brought them

pages.text:
"You're a great detective, Isla!" Marcus said.

"And a great friend to animals," Elena added.

Isla felt proud. She had solved her first real mystery! The baby squirrels were happy and full. The mama squirrel chittered softly, as if saying "thank you."

"Every good detective needs to be kind too," Isla said. "The mama squirrel wasn't being bad. She was being a good mom."

From that day on, Isla left food for the squirrel family every week. And she never had another missing treat mystery to solve. But she was ready for the next adventure!

illustration.description: Isla stands proudly with her detective badge gleaming while Marcus and Elena smile beside her. In the background, the happy squirrel family continues eating peacefully. The scene is celebratory with bright, cheerful colors and a sense of accomplishment and friendship filling the forest clearing.
illustration.alt: Isla celebrates solving her first mystery with her friends as the squirrel family thrives

metadata.illustrationStyle: Playful storybook illustration style. Rich saturated colors. Expressive characters with big eyes. Whimsical, like Pixar concept art.

questions.question: What did Isla find missing from the pet store?
questions.explanation: All the animal treats were missing from the treat cabinet, which started the mystery.

questions.question: What clue did Isla find on the floor?
questions.explanation: Isla found tiny brown crumbs that made a trail leading toward the back door.

questions.question: Who took the treats?
questions.explanation: A mother squirrel took the treats to try to feed her three baby squirrels.

questions.question: Why did the squirrel take the treats?
questions.explanation: The mother squirrel took the treats because she wanted to feed her hungry baby squirrels.

questions.question: How did Isla help the squirrel family?
choices: She brought them proper soft food
questions.explanation: Isla brought soft kitten food, nuts, and fruit that were better for the baby squirrels to eat.

pages.title: Fill in the Missing Words
pages.instructions: Complete the sentences from the story by choosing the correct word for each blank.

sentences.text: Isla worked at the pet store with her _____.
All the animal _____ were missing from the cabinet.
Isla found tiny brown _____ on the floor.

pages.title: More Missing Words
pages.instructions: Keep filling in the blanks to complete more sentences from Isla's mystery.

sentences.text: The mother _____ had taken the treats for her babies.
Isla brought _____ food that was better for baby squirrels.
Isla was a great _____ who solved the mystery.
